papers.title: Scalable deposition and drying methods toward large-area monolithic perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: A systematic analysis of scalable deposition and drying methods for manufacturing large-area monolithic perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells, guiding their transition from research to industry.

papers.title: On the role of thermo-electro-ionic dynamics in hysteresis and transient performance of perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Self-heating interaction with ion migration induces scan-rate-dependent temperature and thermal hysteresis due to comparable thermal, electrical, and ionic timescales, reshaping transient performance and stability in perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Stabilizing the Buried Interface via Inorganic-Organic Hybrid Layers for High-Performance Perovskite Photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Inverted perovskite solar cells, owing to their excellent stability and high compatibility with tandem technologies, have emerged as the most commercially promising new-generation photovoltaic technology. Self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) serve as...

papers.title: Supramolecular hosts for high-performance and sustainable perovskite optoelectronics
papers.abstract: This review outlines the integration of diverse supramolecular hosts into perovskites, achieving control over structure, defects and ion dynamics, advancing next-generation optoelectronics with superior performance, longevity and sustainability.

papers.title: Challenging conventional assumptions in PV: a high-throughput open-air approach to low-cost perovskite module production
papers.abstract: High-throughput open-air manufactured perovskite solar modules achieve target $0.03/kWh levelized cost of energy with the lowest module lifetime barrier of any solar technology, projecting commercial viability with realistic lifetime assumptions.

papers.title: Photostable wide-bandgap perovskites with enhanced interface coupling for all-perovskite tandems
papers.abstract: A molecular bridging strategy is employed to reinforce the chemical and electronic coupling across the hole-collecting SAM/perovskite interface, enabling photostable 1.76 eV perovskites and all-perovskite tandem solar cells with 29.58% efficiency.

papers.title: In situ surface matrix solidification of FAPbI 3 perovskite quantum dots for solar cells with 19.37% efficiency
papers.abstract: An in situ dual ion solidification strategy is reported to renovate the defective surface matrix of perovskite quantum dots, resulting in solar cells with a PCE of up to 19.37%.

papers.title: Closing the gaps: multifunctional molecular filling for highly efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Integrating a multifunctional molecule into a self-assembled hole-transporting layer inhibits ion and SAM migration, enabling perovskite solar cells with superior photovoltaic performance and environmental robustness.

papers.title: Photothermal-stable pure-iodide wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells with suppressed ionic losses
papers.abstract: DMA-stabilized pure-iodide 1.68 eV wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells suppress ion migration through steric hindrance, delivering record stability and meeting a critical lifetime requirement for 25-year operation.

papers.title: A regenerable redox mediator for all-air processed wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells under high-humidity conditions
papers.abstract: We introduce 4-mercaptophenylacetic acid into a perovskite precursor and fabricate devices in ambient air (65% RH), achieving a PCE of 23.16%.

papers.title: Bio-based encapsulation materials enabling recyclable and stable perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: We developed PTFF, a bio-based encapsulation polymeric material that is compatible with existing hot-pressing processes, is non-destructive to perovskites, and is closed-loop recyclable.

papers.title: In situ polymerized zwitterionic elastomers for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: A multifunctional zwitterionic additive regulates crystallization, passivates defects, suppresses ion migration and relaxes residual stress in inverted perovskite solar cells, achieving 26.69% certified efficiency and excellent stability.

papers.title: Orthogonal binary cation arrangement homogenizing interfacial coordination and improving electronic landscapes for high performance perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Orthogonal binary cation passivation, via experimental and theoretical validation, homogenizes perovskite lattice and optoelectronic properties and improves interfacial electronic landscapes, thereby enhancing solar cell efficiency and stability.

papers.title: Review of module designs for organic and perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: This review article discusses prominent patterning methods and module architectures for the scalable fabrication of organic and perovskite solar cell modules.

papers.title: Dynamic reconfiguration of hydrogen-bonded networks to modulate perovskite crystallization
papers.abstract: Sterically tuned DESs reconfigure dynamic H-bond networks to control perovskite crystallization. Replacing the H-bond donor urea with N -methylurea strengthens localized coordination, stabilizes precursors, and yields uniform, low-defect films.

papers.title: Dielectric molecular-bridges enable durable inverted perovskite solar cells with 26.60% efficiency and a high reverse breakdown voltage
papers.abstract: Bis(4-fluorophenyl)chlorophosphine was introduced for increasing perovskite dielectric constant, delivering 26.60% efficiency with a maximum transient reverse breakdown voltage of −6.6 V for perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: The critical role of surface dipoles in CsPbI 3 perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Finely modulating the Cs : Pb ratio of the top 1 nm of CsPbI 3 leads to strong surface dipoles that alter the work function by more than 2 eV, increasing the power conversion efficiency from ∼10% to ∼20% and critically affecting stability.

papers.title: Exploring a scalable route for efficient flexible perovskite solar cells via an amphiphilic cross-linkable monomer
papers.abstract: An amphiphilic monomer for scalable flexible perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Synchronizing crystallization enables thermally stable all-FA Pb–Sn perovskites for printable MA-free all-perovskite tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: Monolithic MA-free all-perovskite tandem solar cells based on lead–tin (Pb–Sn) perovskites have advanced rapidly in recent years.

papers.title: Metallocenium salts as tunable dopants for enhanced efficiency of perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: The generation of free carriers through doping is essential for transforming the electronic properties of organic semiconductors (OSCs). We show that metallocenium salts facilitate OSC doping, enabling high-performance perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Blocking multifaceted degradation pathways via fluorinated hydrogen-bond armor for stable perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: The fluorinated hydrogen-bond armor strategy was proposed to comprehensively protect formamidinium cations from deprotonation. The device achieved high efficiency of 26.51% and exceptional durability under ISOS- l -2 and ISOS- d -2 protocols.

papers.title: Multiphosphorylated molecules for buried interface regulation of inverted perovskite solar cells in a two-step process
papers.abstract: Based on successful construction of a high coverage, uniformly distributed SAM layer and boundary flattening of the perovskite buried interface, we achieve an impressive PCE of 26.27% for inverted PSCs.

papers.title: Cr–O–In interlocking for window layer delamination resistance in operationally stable perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: The thermal evaporation of the Cr film prevents ambient air exposure and induces n-type doping in C 60 , thereby improving interfacial adhesion.

papers.title: Chemical bridging in a 2D/3D heterojunction via dual-anchoring functionalized molecules for efficient, stable and flexible perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Me-TACl modification of the upper interface constructs stable 2D perovskites and forms strong 2D/3D interface coupling, enabling the preparation of high-performance perovskite solar cells with one stone and two birds.

papers.title: Towards space compatible perovskite solar cells: guidelines for thermal shock resilience and near space balloon testing
papers.abstract: In response to the lack of standardized space testing, this work establishes an accelerated thermal shock protocol to rigorously evaluate the stability of space-compatible perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Deprotonated self-assembled molecules as robust hole-selective layers for perovskite/organic tandem solar cells and photocathodes
papers.abstract: Deprotonation-driven molecular interfaces (2PACz-K) enable robust hole-selective layers for efficient perovskite/organic tandem solar cells and transparent, metal-free tandem photocathodes.

papers.title: Beyond rapid nucleation: unveiling the role of solvent–precursor interactions in antisolvent-free perovskite fabrication
papers.abstract: Based on the machine learning-selected solvent, we successfully suppressed the fast crystallization of antisolvent-free perovskites fabricated at high ambient temperature and widened the temperature processing window.

papers.title: Universal non-destructive interconnection layer engineering for efficient and stable perovskite tandem photovoltaics
papers.abstract: This work reveals moisture-induced perovskite damage from aqueous PEDOT:PSS. An ICL decoupling strategy is used to directly study degradation. An isopropanol-dispersible PEDOT enables efficient and stable perovskite-based tandem solar cells.

papers.title: Laser-induced surface hydroxylation of nickel oxide boosting monolayer assembly for high-performance perovskite solar modules
papers.abstract: The laser-induced hydroxylation strategy was applied to strengthen SAMs anchoring on the sputtered NiOx films. The large-area modules achieved high PCE of 22.70% (certified as 22.01%) and exceptional operational durability under ISOS-D-2 protocols.

papers.title: Room temperature buried molecular engineering boosts the photovoltaic performance of wide-bandgap and all-perovskite tandems
papers.abstract: GBAM is used as buried interface modifier at room temperature. Co-annealing GBAM with perovskite layer optimizes bottom interface of wide-bandgap perovskite, achieving 29.07% for 2-terminal perovskite–perovskite tandem cells.

papers.title: Efficient solar-driven hydrogen peroxide production enabled by a perovskite electrochemical device integrated with a cobalt-based chiral catalyst
papers.abstract: Efficient H 2 O 2 production was achieved on chiral Co NH catalyst, enhancing 2e − ORR by removing oxygen intermediate. A Co NH decorated perovskite cathode showed −18 mA cm −2 at 1.2 V RHE , and when paired with an IOR catalyst, delivered 9.4% SCC.

papers.title: Enhanced interface regulation via π-conjugated heterojunctions for high-efficiency inverted perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Incorporating BzMIMBF 4 layer into Me-4PACz (SAM) boosts the perovskite/HTL interface via strong π-conjugated heterojunctions, passivating perovskite defects and thus resulting in stable PSCs with a high PCE of up to 26.45%.

papers.title: Stable perovskite–organic tandem solar cells enabled by chloride-doped evaporated wide-bandgap perovskites
papers.abstract: Based on co-evaporated wide-bandgap perovskites, ∼1% PbCl 2 vapor additive is added to suppress phase segregation and ionic losses, leading to record-stable perovskite–organic tandem cell compared to other kinds of perovskite-based thin-film tandems.

papers.title: Synergistic regulation of crystallization and the buried interface in Cs x FA 1− x PbI 3 perovskite photovoltaics using a multifunctional sulfonyl–ammonium additive
papers.abstract: A multifunctional AEMS additive synergistically regulates the crystallization and buried interface of inverted Cs x FA 1− x PbI 3 perovskite solar cells, yielding a certified efficiency of 26.80% and robust long-term operational stability.

papers.title: Electrochemical quantification of phosphonic acid passivated surface sites of NiO x for perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: A new electrochemical metric quantifies detrimental redox activity in nickel oxide hole transport layers, directly linking surface reactivity to solar cell efficiency and guiding targeted passivation for stable, high-performance photovoltaics.

papers.title: Multidentate silane bridging for stable and efficient perovskite–organic tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: Periodic Pb–O arrays in situ formed on the perovskite surface, effectively passivating Pb 2+ defects, homogenizing energetics, and suppressing ion migration.

papers.title: A simultaneous synergistic protection mechanism in hybrid perovskite–organic multi-junctions enables long-term stable and efficient tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: Monolithic perovskite–organic tandem solar cells (P–O TSCs) establish a mutual protection system, which enables high-efficiency P–O TSCs (25.12%) to achieve exceptional operational stability, retaining over 91% after 1000-hour illumination.

papers.title: Progress and outlooks of large-area flexible organic photovoltaic modules
papers.abstract: Progress and outlooks of large-area flexible organic photovoltaic modules.

papers.title: Correction: Atmospheric-moisture-driven evaporative cooling and concurrent hydrovoltaic energy harvesting in photovoltaic panels
papers.abstract: Correction for ‘Atmospheric-moisture-driven evaporative cooling and concurrent hydrovoltaic energy harvesting in photovoltaic panels’ by Sunmiao Fang et al. , Energy Environ. Sci. , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5ee05530j.

papers.title: Atmospheric-moisture-driven evaporative cooling and concurrent hydrovoltaic energy harvesting in photovoltaic panels
papers.abstract: This work synergistically integrates photovoltaic and hydrovoltaic technologies, simultaneously boosting photovoltaic efficiency and hydrovoltaic output by reclaiming waste heat, providing a blueprint for self-powered energy systems.

papers.title: Mitigating photon escape in thin-film photovoltaic devices via non-reciprocal optical path
papers.abstract: Subwavelength periodic pyramids with high-refractive index generate broadband non-reciprocal optical path, mitigating the trade-off between light harvesting and charge extraction and pushing efficiency of organic solar cells to 20.70%.

papers.title: Unsupervised and few-shot segmentation in photovoltaic electroluminescence images for defect detection via a novel enhanced iterative autoencoder with simple implementation
papers.abstract: PVEL images are particularly valuable for ML-based automated fault detection. A novel EI-AE incorporates an iterative mechanism into the conventional AE framework, providing simple, effective unsupervised pixel-level defect segmentation.

papers.title: Synergistic steric–dipole modulation via stepwise trifluoromethyl substitution enables active-layer hierarchical assembly and >20% power conversion efficiency in organic photovoltaic devices
papers.abstract: A stepwise trifluoromethyl substitution strategy is developed for the structural modification of small-molecule acceptors. The device based on –C(CF 3 ) 3 functionalized CHE-9F achieves an efficiency of 20.30%.

papers.title: Low-temperature all-vacuum-deposited interfacial layers for high-performance and reproducible flexible inverted organic solar cells and modules
papers.abstract: Low-temperature, all-vacuum-deposited interlayers enable precise thickness and uniformity over large areas, achieving rigid and flexible module efficiencies of 17.6% and 15.2% with high reproducibility, advancing flexible OPV commercialization.

papers.title: Breaking the efficiency bottleneck of inverted organic solar cells by reversed sequential deposition
papers.abstract: Reversed LBL strategy based on polymer scaffold with small-molecule interstitial filling enables ideal active layer morphology, achieving >19% efficiency and exceptional stability in inverted organic solar cells with broad material applicability.

papers.title: Two-step templating strategy enabled 20.70% efficiency in layer-by-layer organic solar cells
papers.abstract: A two-step templating strategy, including plasticing and rinsing, is developed to regulate the stacking of bottom layer and deposition dynamics to realize an optimal vertical phase-separation morphology, delivering an outstanding PCE of 20.70%.

papers.title: Hydrogen-bond-driven synergistic regulation of crystallization and interfacial coupling in 1.85 eV wide-bandgap perovskites for high-performance organic tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: Propanedioic acid modulated 1.85 eV WBG perovskite crystallization and reinforced interfacial passivation via hydrogen bonds and coordination, yielding 19.35% efficiency for WBG perovskite devices and 26.25% for perovskite/organic tandems.

papers.title: Tannic acid as a multifunctional bridge driving structural ordering and surface passivation of ZnO for over 20% efficiency inverted solar cells with improved stability
papers.abstract: Tannic acid, a natural polyphenol, reduces structural disorder and passivates surface defects in ZnO transport layers, leading to a record efficiency of 20.1% in inverted organic solar cells and extending the device lifetime from 93 to 2808 hours.

papers.title: Conformational locking through intramolecular F⋯H interactions in dimerized M-series acceptors boosts efficiency and stability of organic solar cells
papers.abstract: Dual F⋯H interactions in M-series dimers enhance molecular order, yielding 19.16% efficiency with outstanding thermal stability.

papers.title: Intrinsic defect compensation in the space charge region enables cadmium-free kesterite solar cells to achieve 13.9% certified efficiency
papers.abstract: Ventilated-air junction heat treatment (JHT) promotes Zn diffusion across the CZTSSe/ZTO interface, leading to intrinsic defect compensation and optimized band alignment, thereby enabling a certified PCE of 13.90% in Cd-free CZTSSe solar cells.

papers.title: Dual-molecule reciprocal doping strategy for cathode interfacial materials enables over 20.7% efficiency in organic solar cells
papers.abstract: A dual-molecule reciprocal doping strategy combined with cooperative molecular assembly forms a charge transport network, enabling a champion efficiency of 20.72% in ternary organic solar cells.

papers.title: 20.46% efficient organic solar cells with concurrent voltage enhancement and thermal stability enabled by crystallization-kinetics-controlled morphology
papers.abstract: Incorporating BTP-FCIO extends the acceptor phase nucleation time from 132 ms to 199 ms and enhances the efficiency to 20.46%. This ternary strategy demonstrates consistent effectiveness across multiple representative Y-series acceptors.

papers.title: Enhanced ε r / L D and improved acceptor crystal growth enable all-polymer solar cells with outstanding efficiency, stability, and processability from a chlorinated-dimer diluent additive
papers.abstract: A chlorinated dimer acceptor (D-SeV-Cl) was designed as a diluent additive for PM6:PYT. Blade-coated all-PSCs using a green solvent achieved a device efficiency PCE exceeding 20%with excellent stability and processability.

papers.title: Charge recombination in polythiophene: non-fullerene acceptor solar cells with IE offsets exceeding 1 eV
papers.abstract: The bi-molecular recombination rate ( γ ) of charge carriers in P3HT:NFA blends decreases gradually as the diagonal bandgap (IE D –EA A ) increases.

papers.title: A non-destructive UV Raman characterisation platform to enable insight into the mechanism of reversible ultraviolet-induced degradation (UVID) in TOPCon solar cells
papers.abstract: A non-destructive UV Raman characterisation platform is developed to reveal reversible changes at the materials level providing better understanding of the mechanism of reversible UVID in TOPCon solar cells.

papers.title: Thermodynamic inhibition of bromine-rich phase nucleation in wide-bandgap perovskites for operationally stable tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: We report a preemptive stabilization strategy for wide-bandgap perovskites, where K + suppresses Br-rich nucleation thermodynamically and synergizes with SCN − to form a 2D phase at grain boundaries, enabling efficient and stable tandem solar cells.

papers.title: 3D imaging-informed electrode engineering for water splitting
papers.abstract: Operando micro-CT and lattice Boltzmann simulations uncover how 3D pore architectures control bubble evolution during water electrolysis, establishing structural design rules for high-performance alkaline water electrolysis.

papers.title: Inverted perovskite solar cells: from fundamentals to scalable commercialization
papers.abstract: This review highlights recent advances in highly efficient and stable inverted PSCs and discusses key directions for achieving large-area fabrication and enhanced stability to accelerate commercialization.

papers.title: Recent advances in stable halide perovskite nanocrystals for optoelectronic devices, biological imaging, and X-ray detection
papers.abstract: This review outlines halide perovskite nanocrystal stabilization strategies via ion-doping, heterostructures, core/shell and surface modification, applications in optoelectronics, biology, and X-ray detection, and discusses challenges and prospects.

papers.title: The long road to outdoor stability: real-world challenges for controlling perovskite materials for solar cells
papers.abstract: Real-world performance of perovskite solar cells: chemical control of materials for highly stable devices under outdoor evaluations.

papers.title: Correction: The long road to outdoor stability: real-world challenges for controlling perovskite materials for solar cells
papers.abstract: Correction for ‘The long road to outdoor stability: real-world challenges for controlling perovskite materials for solar cells’ by Juan José Patiño López et al. , Chem. Soc. Rev. , 2026, 55 , 4648–4706, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5cs01085c.

papers.title: Ternary organic solar cells: optimizing the third components
papers.abstract: This review highlights recent advances in T-OSCs based on NFAs, exploring material design, operational mechanisms, and morphology control to achieve efficiencies of >21%. Critical challenges and pathways toward commercial viability are discussed.

papers.title: From solar cells to memristors: halide perovskites as a platform for neuromorphic electronics
papers.abstract: Halide perovskites, with tunable ionic-electronic properties and strong photoresponse, are apt for optoelectronic memristors. The path to neuromorphic applications- addressing the challenges in materials, devices, and systems, is detailed.

papers.title: Correction: From solar cells to memristors: halide perovskites as a platform for neuromorphic electronics
papers.abstract: Correction for ‘From solar cells to memristors: halide perovskites as a platform for neuromorphic electronics’ by Natalia Yantara et al. , Chem. Soc. Rev. , 2026, 55 , 2145–2228, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5CS01222H.

papers.title: The edge of halide perovskites: surfaces and interfaces drive solar efficiency and stability
papers.abstract: Halide perovskites are promising for next-generation optoelectronic applications, but further efficiency gains are limited by interfaces. This review discusses key interfacial roles, materials design strategies, and advances in characterization.

papers.title: Sustainable Self‐Healing of Perovskite Solar Cells Using Dendrimers as Volatile Reservoirs (Adv. Mater. 5/2026)
Enhancing Stability: Two‐Dimensional Thermochromic Perovskite for Smart Windows in Building Applications (Adv. Funct. Mater. 6/2026)
Ultrastable Perovskite Encased in a Helical Cage for Tunable Full‐Color Mirror‐Image Circularly Polarized Luminescence (Adv. Funct. Mater. 4/2026)
Efficient and Scalable Radiative Cooling for Photovoltaics Using Solution‐Processable and Solar‐Transparent Mesoporous Nanoparticles (Adv. Funct. Mater. 6/2026)
Liquid Nitrogen Quenching‐Induced Orbital Occupancy Modulation for Optimized Intermediate Adsorption for Efficient Anion Exchange Membrane Water Electrolyzers
papers.abstract: ABSTRACT The inherent stress in heterostructures destabilizes the crystal lattice, distorts intermediate adsorption, and increases the energy barrier of the potential‐determining step in anion exchange membrane water electrolyzers (AEMWE). Herein, an innovative liquid nitrogen quenching strategy is developed to induce a high‐spin to low‐spin transition in NiS x ‐based heterostructures through nitrogen incorporation, where the electron‐withdrawing effect modifies the local electronic valence state to optimize intermediate adsorption/desorption energetics and significantly reduce the energy barrier of the potential‐determining step. Consequently, the low‐spin state NiS x nanosheets demonstrate superior electrocatalytic performance, delivering low overpotentials of 78.1 mV for hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) and 210.0 mV for oxygen evolution reaction (OER) at ±10 mA cm −2 , respectively, while enabling efficient overall water splitting with a cell voltage of 1.59 V at 10 mA cm −2 . Meanwhile, the AEMWE with LNQ‐NiS x /NF anode maintains stable operation at 1.25 A cm −2 for over 150 h, showing merely 1.13 mV h −1 potential degradation. In situ characterization confirms robust structural stability and enhanced adsorption of reactive intermediates, while density functional theory (DFT) calculations verify the critical role of low‐spin state in reducing energy barriers. Overall, this work provides a rational design strategy for advanced water electrolyzer electrocatalysts by elucidating the spin state‐activity relationship.

papers.title: Enhanced Thermal Stability in Perovskite Solar Cells via the Integration of a Nonionic Binary Compound (Adv. Energy Mater. 2/2026)
